title: fourSwitchBuckBoost
description: 4-Switch Buck-Boost (also called non-inverting buck-boost, H-bridge buck-boost, FSBB) excitation. Single inductor with two half-bridges (Q1/Q2 buck leg, Q3/Q4 boost leg). The controller selects one of three operating regions per Vin value: BUCK (Vo/Vin < 1-hysteresis: Q3 always-on, Q1/Q2 PWM), BOOST (Vo/Vin > 1+hysteresis: Q1 always-on, Q3/Q4 PWM), or BUCK_BOOST (transition band: all four switches modulate). The magnetic returned by process_design_requirements is the single inductor sized for the worst-case region across the Vin sweep (max(L_buck@VinMax, L_boost@VinMin)). Cin and Co are emitted via get_extra_components_inputs. References: TI SLVA535B, TI LM5175/LM5176, ADI LT8390, Restrepo IEEE 8986423.

inputVoltage.description: The input voltage of the 4-switch buck-boost (must support a sweep range that crosses Vo)

maximumSwitchCurrent.description: The maximum current that can go through any of the four switches

currentRippleRatio.description: The maximum inductor current ripple ratio (delta-IL / IL_avg)

outputVoltageRippleRatio.description: The output voltage ripple ratio (delta-Vo / Vo)

controlMode.description: Controller duty-assignment strategy in the buck-boost region (peakCurrent: TI LM5176, peakBuckPeakBoost: ADI LT8390, averageCurrent: USB-PD, voltageMode: legacy)

transitionMode.description: Buck-boost-region modulation: 'simultaneous' (Mode 1, Q1+Q3 ON together) or 'splitPwm' (Mode 2, sequential split-PWM, LM5176/LT8390 default)

bidirectional.description: If true, controller allows reverse power flow (V2G / battery / 12V<->48V mild-hybrid)

phaseCount.description: Number of interleaved phases (default 1; >1 for automotive 12V<->48V)

transitionHysteresisRatio.description: Width of the buck-boost band as |1 - Vo/Vin| (default 0.15 per LM5176)

fourSwitchBuckBoostOperatingPoint.description: The description of one 4-switch buck-boost operating point. Outputs are non-inverting (Vo positive).
fourSwitchBuckBoostOperatingPoint.title: fourSwitchBuckBoostOperatingPoint
